etymology.description: 練(練) (liàn, "practice; refine") means to practice or train, often implying the refining or improving of skills. 習(習) (xí, "practice; habit") involves repetition or habit, often employed in the context of learning something through regular repetition.
etymology.interpretation: 練(練) (liàn, "practice; refine") conveys the idea of honing skills through disciplined training, likened to refining raw materials into something more valuable. 習(習) (xí, "practice; habit") emphasizes the repetitive aspect, akin to mastering a skill through regular use which becomes habitual. Thus, 練習(練習) (liànxí, "practice") combines the ideas of active training and repetitive learning, resulting in the improvement and internalization of skills.

examples.chinese: 每天练习是提高技能的关键(每天練習是提高技能的關鍵)。
examples.pinyin: Měitiān liànxí shì tígāo jìnéng de guānjiàn.
examples.english: Practicing every day is key to improving skills.

examples.chinese: 她在为比赛认真练习(她在為比賽認真練習)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài wèi bǐsài rènzhēn liànxí.
examples.english: She is practicing diligently for the competition.

examples.chinese: 孩子们喜欢在放学后练习钢琴(孩子們喜歡在放學後練習鋼琴)。
examples.pinyin: Háizimen xǐhuān zài fàngxué hòu liànxí gāngqín.
examples.english: The children like to practice piano after school.
